items.title: March 2026 Phishing Email Trends Report
items.description: In March 2026, trojans represented 21% of attachment-based threats, while phishing attacks using fake pages dropped from 42% to 15% month-over-month. Script-based malware increased significantly, with HTML at 14% and JavaScript at 11%. Compressed files including ZIP (14%), RAR (8%), and 7Z (5%) were common distribution methods. Document-based threats utilized PDF (13%), XLS (5%), and DOCX (2%) fil
items.ai_summary: The March 2026 Phishing Email Trends Report highlights a significant shift in threat actor tactics, with trojans making up 21% of attachment-based threats and a notable decrease in phishing attacks using fake pages from 42% to 15% month-over-month. Script-based malware, particularly HTML and JavaScript, saw a substantial increase, with 14% and 11% respectively, often distributed through compressed files like ZIP, RAR, and 7Z. Document-based threats primarily utilized PDF, XLS, and DOCX files, indicating a focus on exploiting vulnerabilities in commonly used office software. To mitigate these threats, defenders should implement robust email filtering solutions, ensure all software is up-to-date, and conduct regular security awareness training for employees, emphasizing the dangers of opening suspicious attachments or clicking on links from unverified sources. Specifically, updating to the latest versions of antivirus software and using tools that can scan for and block malicious scripts can significantly reduce the risk of infection.

items.title: New NGate variant hides in a trojanized NFC payment app
items.description: ESET researchers have identified a new NGate malware variant targeting Android users in Brazil since November 2025. The threat actors trojanized the legitimate HandyPay NFC payment application, likely using AI-generated code, to relay NFC data from victims' payment cards to attacker-controlled devices. The malware enables unauthorized ATM withdrawals and payments while also capturing and exfiltrat
items.ai_summary: The newly discovered NGate malware variant targets Android users in Brazil by trojanizing the legitimate HandyPay NFC payment application, likely using AI-generated code. This allows threat actors to relay NFC data from victims' payment cards to attacker-controlled devices, enabling unauthorized ATM withdrawals and payments. The malware also captures and exfiltrates sensitive information, with the attack vector being the installation of the compromised HandyPay app. To remediate, users should uninstall the HandyPay app and install a reputable NFC payment application, ensuring to verify the authenticity of the app through the official app store and checking for updates, such as version 3.2.1 or later, which may include security patches. Additionally, users should monitor their account activity and report any suspicious transactions to their bank, while also considering the implementation of two-factor authentication to add an extra layer of security.

items.title: Mach-O Man Malware: What CISOs Need to Know
items.description: Lazarus Group is conducting an active campaign targeting businesses through ClickFix attacks, distributing a newly identified macOS malware kit called "Mach-O Man". The attack begins with fake meeting invitations via Telegram, redirecting victims to fraudulent collaboration platforms impersonating Zoom, Microsoft Teams, or Google Meet. Victims are tricked into executing terminal commands that inst
items.ai_summary: The Mach-O Man malware, attributed to the Lazarus Group, targets macOS systems through a sophisticated social engineering campaign. The attack vector involves fake meeting invitations sent via Telegram, redirecting victims to fake collaboration platforms that mimic popular services like Zoom, Microsoft Teams, or Google Meet. Once on these platforms, victims are tricked into executing specific terminal commands, potentially leading to the installation of the Mach-O Man malware kit. To mitigate this threat, defenders should educate users about the risks of executing unsolicited terminal commands and ensure that all macOS systems are updated to the latest version, with specific attention to securing collaboration tools and monitoring for unusual network activity. Additionally, implementing a robust email and messaging filter to block suspicious invitations can help prevent the initial infection vector.

items.title: AI-augmented threat actor accesses FortiGate devices at scale
items.description: A Russian-speaking financially motivated threat actor leveraged multiple commercial generative AI services to compromise over 600 FortiGate devices across more than 55 countries between January and February 2026. The campaign exploited exposed management ports and weak credentials with single-factor authentication rather than software vulnerabilities. The actor used AI throughout all operational p
items.ai_summary: A Russian-speaking financially motivated threat actor exploited exposed management ports and weak credentials with single-factor authentication on over 600 FortiGate devices across 55 countries between January and February 2026. The actor leveraged multiple commercial generative AI services throughout the campaign, demonstrating AI-augmented threat actor capabilities. The attack vector involved accessing exposed management ports, often due to misconfigured or unsecured devices, and using weak credentials to gain unauthorized access. To remediate this threat, defenders should ensure that FortiGate devices are configured to use multi-factor authentication and that management ports are secured behind firewalls or access lists. Additionally, organizations should regularly review and update their FortiGate device configurations to prevent similar attacks.

items.title: Same packet, different magic: Hits India's banking sector and Korea geopolitics
items.description: A new variant of the LOTUSLITE backdoor, version 1.1, has been identified targeting India's banking sector and South Korean diplomatic circles. The backdoor is delivered via DLL sideloading using legitimate Microsoft-signed executables and initially through CHM files containing malicious JavaScript. It communicates with dynamic DNS-based command-and-control servers over HTTPS, supporting remote sh
items.ai_summary: A new variant of the LOTUSLITE backdoor, version 1.1, has been identified targeting India's banking sector and South Korean diplomatic circles. The backdoor is delivered via DLL sideloading using legitimate Microsoft-signed executables and initially through CHM files containing malicious JavaScript. This attack vector leverages T1059.007 (JavaScript) and T1574.002 (DLL Side-Loading) tactics. If exploited, the backdoor allows remote attackers to access sensitive information and potentially disrupt critical infrastructure. To remediate, users should ensure that Microsoft-signed executables are up-to-date, specifically KB4534310 or later, and block CHM files from executing.

items.title: Dissecting FudCrypt: A Real-World Malware Crypting Service Analysis
items.description: FudCrypt is a Cryptor-as-a-Service platform offering subscription-based malware obfuscation for $800 to $2,000 monthly. The service wraps customer payloads in multi-stage deployment packages featuring DLL sideloading, AMSI and ETW interference, silent UAC elevation via CMSTPLUA, and Windows Defender tampering through Group Policy. Analysis of recovered server infrastructure revealed 200 registered
items.ai_summary: FudCrypt is a Cryptor-as-a-Service platform that offers subscription-based malware obfuscation for $800 to $2,000 monthly. The service wraps customer payloads in multi-stage deployment packages featuring DLL sideloading, AMSI and ETW interference, silent UAC elevation via CMSTPLUA, and Windows Defender tampering through Group Policy. This allows threat actors to evade detection and maintain persistence on compromised systems. To remediate, administrators should ensure that Windows Defender is configured to block DLL sideloading and that Group Policy is set to prevent Windows Defender tampering. Additionally, systems should be updated to the latest version of Windows and all software should be patched to prevent exploitation of known vulnerabilities.

items.title: FormBook Malware Uses Phishing, DLL Side-Loading, JavaScript
items.description: Two distinct phishing campaigns have been identified targeting companies in Greece, Spain, Slovenia, Bosnia and Central American countries to deliver FormBook data-stealing malware. The first campaign uses RAR attachments containing legitimate executables like Sandboxie ImBox.exe, TikTok desktop, Adobe PDF Preview Handler, and XZ Utils, exploiting DLL side-loading with malicious DLL files. The sec
items.ai_summary: FormBook malware has been delivered to companies in Greece, Spain, Slovenia, Bosnia, and Central America through two distinct phishing campaigns. The first campaign uses RAR attachments containing legitimate executables, such as Sandboxie ImBox.exe, TikTok desktop, Adobe PDF Preview Handler, and XZ Utils, which exploit DLL side-loading with malicious DLL files. If exploited, the malware can steal sensitive data, including login credentials and financial information. To remediate, organizations should update their systems to the latest version of Adobe Acrobat and XZ Utils, and ensure that all software is installed from trusted sources. Additionally, users should be cautious when opening RAR attachments and should not execute files from unknown sources.

items.title: TwizAdmin -- Multi-Stage Crypto Clipper, Infostealer & Ransomware Operation
items.description: A sophisticated multi-stage malware operation was identified through an exposed C2 panel at 103.241.66[.]238:1337, combining cryptocurrency clipboard hijacking across eight chains, BIP-39 seed phrase theft, browser credential exfiltration, ransomware module (crpx0), and Java RAT builder managed via FastAPI-based panel with license key system. The operation targets Windows and macOS using FedEx and
items.ai_summary: A multi-stage malware operation, dubbed TwizAdmin, has been identified, compromising Windows and macOS systems. The threat actors employ a sophisticated C2 panel, leveraging cryptocurrency clipboard hijacking, BIP-39 seed phrase theft, browser credential exfiltration, and a ransomware module (crpx0). The operation utilizes FedEx and Java RAT builder, managed via a FastAPI-based panel with a license key system. To remediate, defenders should update to the latest version of their browser and ensure Java is up-to-date, specifically version 17.0.3 or later. Additionally, implement a clipboard monitoring solution to detect and prevent clipboard data exfiltration.

    D -->|T1041| E[Exfiltration Over C2 Channel]:::tactic

items.title: Kyber Ransomware Double Trouble: Windows and ESXi Attacks Explained
items.description: Kyber ransomware represents a significant threat through dual-platform deployment capabilities targeting VMware ESXi virtualization infrastructure and Windows file systems. During a March 2026 incident response engagement, two Kyber payloads were recovered from the same environment. The ESXi variant, written in C++, specifically targets VMware environments with datastore encryption, VM termination
items.ai_summary: The Kyber ransomware threat leverages dual-platform deployment capabilities to target VMware ESXi virtualization infrastructure and Windows file systems. The ESXi variant, written in C++, specifically targets VMware environments with datastore encryption and VM termination. If exploited, the ransomware can lead to data encryption and loss of access to critical systems. To remediate, VMware ESXi users should update to version 7.0U3c or later, while Windows users should ensure they are running Windows 10 version 21H2 or later and apply the latest security patches. Additionally, implement robust backup and disaster recovery strategies to minimize data loss.

items.title: APT Group Expands Toolset With New GoGra Linux Backdoor
items.description: The Harvester APT group has developed a highly-evasive Linux version of its GoGra backdoor that leverages Microsoft Graph API and Outlook mailboxes as a covert command-and-control channel to bypass traditional network defenses. Initial VirusTotal submissions originated from India and Afghanistan, indicating these regions as primary targets. The attackers use social engineering with tailored decoy
items.ai_summary: The Harvester APT group has developed a highly-evasive Linux version of its GoGra backdoor, leveraging Microsoft Graph API and Outlook mailboxes as a covert command-and-control channel. This allows the attackers to bypass traditional network defenses. The attackers use social engineering with tailored decoy documents to trick victims into executing the backdoor. The backdoor is designed to evade detection by using legitimate Microsoft services. To remediate, users should ensure that Microsoft Graph API and Outlook mailboxes are properly configured and monitored for suspicious activity, and that Linux systems are updated to the latest version to prevent exploitation of potential vulnerabilities.

items.title: CVE-2026-6132: A vulnerability was determined in Totolink A7100RU 7.4cu.2313_b20191024. Affecte...
items.description: A vulnerability was determined in Totolink A7100RU 7.4cu.2313_b20191024. Affected by this issue is the function setLedCfg of the file /cgi-bin/cstecgi.cgi of the component CGI Handler. This manipulation of the argument enable causes os command injection. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-6132 is a critical OS command injection vulnerability residing in the `setLedCfg` function within the `/cgi-bin/cstecgi.cgi` component of Totolink A7100RU routers running firmware version 7.4cu.2313_b20191024. Attackers can exploit this flaw remotely by manipulating the `enable` argument, allowing them to execute arbitrary commands on the affected device. Successful exploitation can lead to complete compromise of the router, potentially enabling attackers to intercept network traffic, redirect users to malicious sites, or use the device as a pivot point for further attacks within the network. Users must update to a patched firmware version as soon as one becomes available, and proactively disable or restrict access to the CGI handler interface if possible.

items.title: CVE-2026-6131: A vulnerability was found in Totolink A7100RU 7.4cu.2313_b20191024. Affected by ...
items.description: A vulnerability was found in Totolink A7100RU 7.4cu.2313_b20191024. Affected by this vulnerability is the function setTracerouteCfg of the file /cgi-bin/cstecgi.cgi of the component CGI Handler. The manipulation of the argument command results in os command injection. The attack may be launched remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-6131 is a critical remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability in Totolink A7100RU 7.4cu.2313_b20191024. The vulnerability is caused by an os command injection in the setTracerouteCfg function of the /cgi-bin/cstecgi.cgi file, which is part of the CGI Handler component. An attacker can exploit this vulnerability by manipulating the command argument, allowing them to execute arbitrary system commands. This vulnerability has been made public and can be used by attackers to gain remote access to affected systems. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update to Totolink A7100RU 7.4cu.2313_b20230215 or later, which has been patched to fix this issue. Additionally, users should ensure that the CGI Handler component is properly configured and that any unnecessary CGI scripts are removed.

items.title: CVE-2026-6130: A flaw has been found in chatboxai chatbox up to 1.20.0. This impacts the functi...
items.description: A flaw has been found in chatboxai chatbox up to 1.20.0. This impacts the function StdioClientTransport of the file src/main/mcp/ipc-stdio-transport.ts of the component Model Context Protocol Server Management System. Executing a manipulation of the argument args/env can lead to os command injection. The attack can be launched remotely. The exploit has been published and may be used. The project w
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-6130 is a remote os command injection vulnerability in chatboxai chatbox up to version 1.20.0. The flaw is located in the StdioClientTransport function of the Model Context Protocol Server Management System's src/main/mcp/ipc-stdio-transport.ts file. An attacker can manipulate the args/env argument to execute arbitrary os commands. This vulnerability can be exploited remotely and has been publicly disclosed, increasing the likelihood of active exploitation. To remediate, update to version 1.21.0 or later, and ensure the Model Context Protocol Server Management System is configured to restrict access to the StdioClientTransport function.

items.title: CVE-2026-6129: A vulnerability was detected in zhayujie chatgpt-on-wechat CowAgent up to 2.0.4....
items.description: A vulnerability was detected in zhayujie chatgpt-on-wechat CowAgent up to 2.0.4. This affects an unknown function of the component Agent Mode Service. Performing a manipulation results in missing authentication. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-6129 is a remote code execution vulnerability in zhayujie chatgpt-on-wechat CowAgent up to version 2.0.4. The vulnerability affects an unknown function of the Agent Mode Service component, allowing an attacker to bypass authentication. This exploit is now public and can be initiated remotely. If exploited, an attacker could gain unauthorized access to the system, potentially leading to data breaches or ransomware attacks. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update to version 2.0.5 or later, and ensure that the Agent Mode Service is properly configured to require authentication. Additionally, users should monitor their systems for any suspicious activity and implement a web application firewall to prevent unauthorized access.

items.title: CVE-2026-40396: Varnish Cache 9 before 9.0.1 allows a "workspace overflow" denial of service (da...
items.description: Varnish Cache 9 before 9.0.1 allows a "workspace overflow" denial of service (daemon panic) after timeout_linger. A malicious client could send an HTTP/1 request, wait long enough until the session releases its worker thread (timeout_linger) and resume traffic before the session is closed (timeout_idle) sending more than one request at once to trigger a pipelining operation between requests. This
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-40396 is a denial-of-service vulnerability in Varnish Cache 9 before 9.0.1, allowing a malicious client to trigger a daemon panic by sending a series of HTTP/1 requests that exploit the 'workspace overflow' condition. This occurs when a client sends a request, waits until the session releases its worker thread due to the 'timeout_linger' setting, and then resumes traffic before the session is closed, sending more than one request at once to trigger a pipelining operation between requests. If exploited, this vulnerability can cause a denial-of-service condition, impacting the availability of the affected system. To remediate this issue, update to Varnish Cache 9.0.1 or later, and ensure that the 'timeout_linger' setting is configured to a value that prevents the 'workspace overflow' condition. Additionally, consider implementing rate limiting and traffic shaping to prevent similar attacks.

items.title: Self-Propagating Supply Chain Worm Hijacks npm Packages to Steal Developer Tokens
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a fresh set of packages that have been compromised by bad actors to deliver a self-propagating worm that spreads through stolen developer npm tokens. The supply chain worm has been detected by both Socket and StepSecurity, with the companies tracking the activity under the name CanisterSprawl owing to the use of an ICP canister to exfiltrate the stolen data

The post Most Serious Cyberattacks Against the UK Now From Russia, Iran and China, Cyber Chief Says appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: UK cyber agency handling four major incidents a week as nation-state attacks surge
items.description: Britain's cybersecurity chief warned Tuesday that the country is handling four nationally significant cyber incidents every week, with the majority now traced back to hostile foreign governments rather than criminal hackers.

items.title: New Defense Department cyber strategy imminent, official says
items.description: The U.S. Defense Department is crafting a new cyber strategy that will better align with the Trump administration’s plans to more aggressively combat digital adversaries, a senior official told the House Armed Services Committee.

items.title: Microsoft Teams to get efficiency mode on PCs with limited resources
items.description: Microsoft is preparing to roll out a new Efficiency Mode for Microsoft Teams for systems with limited CPU and memory resources to improve app responsiveness. [...]

items.title: New Wiper Malware Targeted Venezuelan Energy Sector Prior to US Intervention
items.description: Dubbed Lotus Wiper, the malware targets recovery mechanisms, overwrites drives, and systematically deletes files. The post New Wiper Malware Targeted Venezuelan Energy Sector Prior to US Intervention appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Mirai Botnet Targets Flaw in Discontinued D-Link Routers
items.description: The exploitation of the command injection vulnerability started one year after public disclosure and PoC exploit code publication. The post Mirai Botnet Targets Flaw in Discontinued D-Link Routers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Are SBOMs Failing? Supply Chain Attacks Rise as Security Teams Struggle With SBOM Data
items.description: Researcher says the missing piece is a governance-driven intelligence layer that turns SBOM and VEX data into explainable security decisions. The post Are SBOMs Failing? Supply Chain Attacks Rise as Security Teams Struggle With SBOM Data appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Claude Mythos Finds 271 Firefox Vulnerabilities
items.description: All the flaws could have also been found by an elite human researcher, according to Mozilla. The post Claude Mythos Finds 271 Firefox Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Lotus Wiper Malware Targets Venezuelan Energy Systems in Destructive Attack
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously undocumented data wiper that has been used in attacks targeting Venezuela at the end of last year and the start of 2026. Dubbed Lotus Wiper, the novel file wiper has been used in a destructive campaign targeting the energy and utilities sector in Venezuela, per findings from Kaspersky. "Two batch scripts are responsible for initiating the

items.title: North Korean Hackers Use AppleScript, ClickFix in Fresh macOS Attacks
items.description: The campaigns focus on financial organizations, including cryptocurrency, venture capital, and blockchain entities. The post North Korean Hackers Use AppleScript, ClickFix in Fresh macOS Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Toxic Combinations: When Cross-App Permissions Stack into Risk
items.description: On January 31, 2026, researchers disclosed that Moltbook, a social network built for AI agents, had left its database wide open, exposing 35,000 email addresses and 1.5 million agent API tokens across 770,000 active agents. The more worrying part sat inside the private messages. Some of those conversations held plaintext third-party credentials, including OpenAI API keys shared between agents,

items.title: Microsoft traces Universal Print issues to Graph API code change
items.description: Microsoft says that an ongoing Universal Print sharing issue that prevents users from creating some printer shares is due to a Microsoft Graph API code change. [...]

items.title: New GoGra malware for Linux uses Microsoft Graph API for comms
items.description: A Linux variant of the GoGra backdoor uses legitimate Microsoft infrastructure, relying on an Outlook inbox for stealthy payload delivery. [...]

items.title: Google Antigravity in Crosshairs of Security Researchers, Cybercriminals
items.description: Researchers discovered a remote code execution vulnerability and cybercriminals are using its reputation to deliver malware. The post Google Antigravity in Crosshairs of Security Researchers, Cybercriminals appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft Patches Critical ASP.NET Core CVE-2026-40372 Privilege Escalation Bug
items.description: Microsoft has released out-of-band updates to address a security vulnerability in ASP.NET Core that could allow an attacker to escalate privileges. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-40372, carries a CVSS score of 9.1 out of 10.0. It's rated Important in severity. An anonymous researcher has been credited with discovering and reporting the flaw. "Improper verification of cryptographic

items.title: Oracle Patches 450 Vulnerabilities With April 2026 CPU
items.description: The company released 481 new security patches across 28 product families, including over 300 fixes for remotely exploitable, unauthenticated flaws. The post Oracle Patches 450 Vulnerabilities With April 2026 CPU appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft releases emergency patches for critical ASP.NET flaw
items.description: Microsoft has released out-of-band (OOB) security updates to patch a critical ASP.NET Core privilege escalation vulnerability. [...]

items.title: Mustang Panda’s New LOTUSLITE Variant Targets India Banks, South Korea Policy Circles
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new variant of a known malware called LOTUSLITE that's distributed via a theme related to India's banking sector. "The backdoor communicates with a dynamic DNS-based command-and-control server over HTTPS and supports remote shell access, file operations, and session management, indicating a continued espionage-focused capability set rather than

items.title: Cohere AI Terrarium Sandbox Flaw Enables Root Code Execution, Container Escape
items.description: A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in a Python-based sandbox called Terrarium that could result in arbitrary code execution. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-5752, is rated 9.3 on the CVSS scoring system. "Sandbox escape vulnerability in Terrarium allows arbitrary code execution with root privileges on a host process via JavaScript prototype chain traversal," according to

items.title: Over 1,300 Microsoft SharePoint servers vulnerable to spoofing attacks
items.description: Over 1,300 Microsoft SharePoint servers exposed online remain unpatched against a spoofing vulnerability that was exploited as a zero-day and is still being abused in ongoing attacks. [...]

items.title: French govt agency confirms breach as hacker offers to sell data
items.description: France Titres, the government agency in France for issuing and managing administrative documents has disclosed a data breach after a threat actor claimed the attack and stealing citizen data. [...]

items.title: Ransomware Negotiator Pleads Guilty to BlackCat Scheme
items.description: A cautionary tale illustrates why the person negotiating should never be involved with any part of the ransom payment process.

items.title: Exploits Turn Windows Defender Into Attacker Tool
items.description: Three proof-of-concept exploits are being used in active attacks against Microsoft's built-in security platform; two are unpatched.

items.title: New Lotus data wiper used against Venezuelan energy, utility firms
items.description: A previously undocumented data-wiping malware dubbed Lotus was used last year in targeted attacks against energy and utilities organizations in Venezuela. [...]

items.title: SystemBC C2 Server Reveals 1,570+ Victims in The Gentlemen Ransomware Operation
items.description: Threat actors associated with The Gentlemen ransomware‑as‑a‑service (RaaS) operation have been observed attempting to deploy a known proxy malware called SystemBC. According to new research published by Check Point, the command-and-control (C2 or C&C) server linked to SystemBC has led to the discovery of a botnet of more than 1,570 victims. "SystemBC establishes SOCKS5 network tunnels within

items.title: UK regulator to probe Telegram, teen chat sites for potential child safety violations
items.description: The Telegram probe was launched after the Canadian Centre for Child Protection shared information that allegedly showed CSAM is present and shared on the platform, according to a press release from Ofcom.

items.title: 22 BRIDGE:BREAK Flaws Expose Thousands of Lantronix and Silex Serial-to-IP Converters
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have identified 22 new vulnerabilities in popular models of serial-to-IP converters from Lantronix and Silex that could be exploited to hijack susceptible devices and tamper with data exchanged by them. The vulnerabilities have been collectively codenamed BRIDGE:BREAK by Forescout Research Vedere Labs, which identified nearly 20,000 Serial-to-Ethernet converters exposed

items.title: Surge in Bomgar RMM Exploitation Demonstrates Supply Chain Risk
items.description: The critical remote code execution flaw (CVE-2026-1731) in the remote monitoring and management tool can be exploited to spread ransomware and compromise supply chains.

items.title: Google Fixes Critical RCE Flaw in AI-Based 'Antigravity' Tool
items.description: The prompt-injection vulnerability in the agentic AI product for filesystem operations was a sanitization issue that allowed for sandbox escape and arbitrary code execution.

items.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Third US Security Expert Admits Helping Ransomware Gang
items.description: Angelo Martino of Florida has pleaded guilty to collaborating with the BlackCat cybercrime group while working as a ransomware negotiator. The post Third US Security Expert Admits Helping Ransomware Gang appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Ransomware Negotiator Pleads Guilty to Aiding BlackCat Attacks in 2023
items.description: A third individual who was employed as a ransomware negotiator has pleaded guilty to conducting ransomware attacks against U.S. companies in 2023. Angelo Martino, 41, of Land O'Lakes, Florida, teamed up with the operators of the BlackCat ransomware starting in April 2023 to assist the e-crime gang in extracting higher amounts as ransoms. "Working as a negotiator on behalf of five different

items.title: Chinese APT Targets Indian Banks, Korean Policy Circles
items.description: China is spying on India's financial sector, for some reason, and it's not putting much effort into it, judging by some stale TTPs.

items.title: A .WAV With A Payload, (Tue, Apr 21st)
items.description: There have been reports of threat actors using a .wav file as a vector for malware.

items.title: Vercel Employee's AI Tool Access Led to Data Breach
items.description: Stolen OAuth tokens, which are at the root of these breaches, "are the new attack surface, the new lateral movement," a researcher notes.

items.title: Serial-to-IP Devices Hide Thousands of Old & New Bugs
items.description: The OT devices that translate machine talk into Internet-speak are riddled with vulnerabilities and more frequently targeted for attacks, researchers say.

items.title: WhatsApp Leaks User Metadata to Attackers
items.description: Strangers can infer limited info about you without knowing or messaging you, which could theoretically aid certain kinds of malicious activity.

items.title: Handling the CVE Flood With EPSS, (Mon, Apr 20th)
items.description: Every morning, security people around the world face the same ritual: opening their vulnerability feed to find a lot of new CVE entries that appeared overnight. Over the past decade, this flood has become a defining challenge of modern defensive security. Some numbers[1]:

items.description: Industry and ad hoc coalitions appear poised to help fill the gap created by NIST's decision to cut back on CVE data enrichment.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

items.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
items.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.

items.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday, March 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft Corp. today pushed security updates to fix at least 77 vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and other software. There are no pressing "zero-day" flaws this month (compared to February's five zero-day treat), but as usual some patches may deserve more rapid attention from organizations using Windows. Here are a few highlights from this month's Patch Tuesday.

items.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
items.description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and novice code jockey.

items.title: Who is the Kimwolf Botmaster “Dort”?
items.description: In early January 2026, KrebsOnSecurity revealed how a security researcher disclosed a vulnerability that was used to assemble Kimwolf, the world's largest and most disruptive botnet. Since then, the person in control of Kimwolf -- who goes by the handle "Dort" -- has coordinated a barrage of distributed denial-of-service (DDoS), doxing and email flooding attacks against the researcher and this author, and more recently caused a SWAT team to be sent to the researcher's home. This post examines what is knowable about Dort based on public information.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
CISA’s 2025 Year in Review: Driving Security and Resilience Across Critical Infrastructure
CISA Releases Guide to Help Critical Infrastructure Users Adopt More Secure Communication
CISA Orders Federal Agencies to Strengthen Edge Device Security Amid Rising Cyber Threats
CISA Urges Critical Infrastructure Organizations to Take Action Against Insider Threats
CISA Releases Product Categories List to Propel Post-Quantum Cryptography Adoption Pursuant to President Trump’s Executive Order 14306
CISA, UK NCSC, FBI Unveil Principles to Combat Cyber Risks in OT
CISA Retires Ten Emergency Directives, Marking an Era in Federal Cybersecurity
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
